content_markdown: For a standing wave, all the particles between two successive nodes have the same

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: In a standing wave, all particles within a single loop (between two adjacent nodes) vibrate in phase with the same frequency. However, the amplitude varies from zero at the nodes to a maximum at the antinode. Since energy is proportional to the square of the amplitude, the energy also varies across the loop.
